title: Magnificently Mashed Irish Potato Gratin
description: A sweet and bouncy Irish dish featuring salted butter and al-dente finish, made in a comal and suitable for an ayurvedic sattvic diet. This show-off dish is perfect for special occasions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large comal, combine the chopped onion and minced garlic and cook over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped potatoes to the comal and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a separate saucepan, heat the milk over low heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the softened salted butter to the milk and stir until it is fully incorporated.
Add the grated cheddar cheese to the milk mixture and stir until it is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Add the cooked potato mixture to the cheese mixture and stir until the potatoes are fully coated.
Season the potato mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the potato mixture to a baking dish and top with additional grated cheese and chopped parsley.
Bake the potato gratin in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are al-dente.
